methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Multiply Labs is a Series A biotech company founded by Fred Parietti and Alice Melocchi that has raised $41M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comparable universe of 11 companies in Biotech & Health—3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private companies at Series B and adjacent stages, including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression, using total raised ($85M) as a proxy base value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implies a valuation of $170M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $805M, with the peer range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median: 7.8x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied, as Multiply Labs' revenue is not on file.

The triangulated valuation range is $144M to $926M, with a median of $488M. The wide dispersion reflects tension between the stage-progression method, which anchors conservatively to near-term capital deployment, and the peer-median method, which reflects valuations of more advanced Series B peers. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, clinical or commercial validation milestones, and whether peer multiples reflect sector-wide froth or genuine risk-adjusted value creation in the biotech frontier space.
